org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism

Interface Anchor<G extends Graph<?,N,A,E>,N extends Node<G,?,A,E>,A extends Anchor<G,N,?,E>,E extends Edge<G,N,A,?>>

  • Type Parameters:
    G - is the type of the graph.
    N - is the type of the node inside the graph.
    A - is the type of the anchors inside the graph.
    E - is the type of the node inside the graph.
    All Superinterfaces:
    Comparable<ModelObject>, ModelObject, Serializable
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractAnchor, FSMAnchor, StandardAnchor

    public interface Anchor<G extends Graph<?,N,A,E>,N extends Node<G,?,A,E>,A extends Anchor<G,N,?,E>,E extends Edge<G,N,A,?>>
extends ModelObject
    This is the connection point that permit to connect Edges and Nodes.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:02:58
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND, Marc BAUMGARTNER
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-formalism

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addEdge(E edge)
      Attached an Edge to this Anchor.
      boolean canConnectAsEndAnchor(E edge, A startAnchor)
      Reply if this anchor can be the end of the specified edge if the beginning is binded to the specified anchor.
      boolean canConnectAsStartAnchor(E edge, A endAnchor)
      Reply if this anchor can be the beginning of the specified edge if the end is binded to the specified anchor.
      List<E> getEdges()
      Reply all Edges attached to this Node by the way of Anchors.
      AnchorLocation getLocation()
      Gets the location of the Anchor according to the owner
      N getNode()
      Get the Node that contains this Anchor.
      void removeAllEdges()
      Removes all Edges attached to this Anchor.
      void removeEdge(E edge)
      Remove an Edge attached to this Anchor.
      void setLocation(AnchorLocation location)
      Sets the location of the Anchor according to the owner The name of the property is "anchorLocation".
      void setNode(N node)
      Set the Node that contains this Anchor.

      • Methods inherited from interface org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.ModelObject

        addModelObjectListener, findModelObject, fireComponentAdded, fireComponentRemoved, fireContainerChanged, fireContentChanged, fireLinkChanged, firePropertyChanged, getMetamodelSpecification, getName, getProperties, getUIEditableProperties, getUUID, getViewBinding, removeModelObjectListener, setName, setProperties, setUUID

      • Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Comparable

        compareTo

    • Method Detail

      • setNode

        void setNode(N node)
        Set the Node that contains this Anchor.
        Parameters:
        node - Node container.

      • getNode

        N getNode()
        Get the Node that contains this Anchor.
        Returns:
        Node container.

      • getEdges

        List<E> getEdges()
        Reply all Edges attached to this Node by the way of Anchors.
        Returns:
        a set of Edges.

      • addEdge

        void addEdge(E edge)
        Attached an Edge to this Anchor.
        Parameters:
        edge - a new Edges.

      • removeEdge

        void removeEdge(E edge)
        Remove an Edge attached to this Anchor.
        Parameters:
        edge - the Edges to remove.

      • removeAllEdges

        void removeAllEdges()
        Removes all Edges attached to this Anchor.
        Since:
        0.2

      • setLocation

        void setLocation(AnchorLocation location)
        Sets the location of the Anchor according to the owner The name of the property is "anchorLocation".
        Parameters:
        location - the location of the Anchor
        Since:
        0.4

      • getLocation

        AnchorLocation getLocation()
        Gets the location of the Anchor according to the owner

        Returns:
        the location of the Anchor
        Since:
        0.4

      • canConnectAsStartAnchor

        boolean canConnectAsStartAnchor(E edge,
                              A endAnchor)
        Reply if this anchor can be the beginning of the specified edge if the end is binded to the specified anchor. By default all edges can be connected to the anchor. Subclasses must override this method to have a more restrictive managing.
        Parameters:
        edge - is the edge to test against this anchor..
        endAnchor - is the anchor in the other side of the given edge. It may be null.
        Returns:
        true if this anchor can be connected to the given edge as a start anchor.

      • canConnectAsEndAnchor

        boolean canConnectAsEndAnchor(E edge,
                            A startAnchor)
        Reply if this anchor can be the end of the specified edge if the beginning is binded to the specified anchor. By default all edges can be connected to the anchor. Subclasses must override this method to have a more restrictive managing.
        Parameters:
        edge - is the edge to test against this anchor..
        startAnchor - is the anchor in the other side of the given edge. It may be null.
        Returns:
        true if this anchor can be connected to the given edge as a start anchor.
